tax: Placed at family rank, Still-Water Fluvials is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a still-water body bound to a sump. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a still-water body with its own pool source.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a standing dark water column. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a windless standing wave and high-water mark — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Still-Water Fluvials occupies flooded sumps and pools. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure animates a sump and recur with the sump refills, sustaining themselves on water-pressure.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: hides as a pool, surges to drown. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: coherent still-water body and pressure-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: surging and drowning. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Still-Water Fluvials matters because makes quiet pools drowning traps. It lets survey reports sort still-water hazards from white-water ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Still-Water Fluvials is the family-rank grouping of deep still-water elementals of sumps and pools within the True Elementals. Its members share a dark coherent still-water body that surges from depth, and divide below into genus groups by sump depth and water chemistry. The drowning hazard of every quiet pool.
